title: Castle Law Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Section 7.2
text: vignette replicates staggered estimates Lee & Wooldridge (2025), Section 7.2, using lwdidR package. analysis studies effect Castle Doctrine laws log homicide rates US states, 2000–2010. Expected results (paper Python replication):

title: Castle Law Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Section 7.2
code:
library(lwdidR) library(knitr) data_path <- system.file("extdata", "castle.csv", package = "lwdidR") stopifnot(nchar(data_path) > 0) castle <- read.csv(data_path)  # Construct gvar: first treatment year (NA for never treated) castle$gvar <- castle$effyear castle$gvar[is.na(castle$gvar) | castle$gvar == 0] <- NA  # Verify data structure cat("Dimensions:", nrow(castle), "x", ncol(castle), "
") ## Dimensions: 550 x 61 cat("Years:", min(castle$year), "to", max(castle$year), "
") ## Years: 2000 to 2010 cat("States:", length(unique(castle$sid)), "
") ## States: 50 cat("Treatment cohorts:
") ## Treatment cohorts: cohort_counts <- table(castle$gvar[!duplicated(castle$sid)], useNA = "ifany") print(cohort_counts) ##  ## 2005 2006 2007 2008 2009 <NA>  ##    1   13    4    2    1   29

title: Castle Law Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Section 7.2
code: res_demean_ols <- lwdid(   data          = castle,   y             = "lhomicide",   ivar          = "sid",   tvar          = "year",   gvar          = "gvar",   rolling       = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate     = "overall",   vce           = NULL  # homoskedastic OLS ) print(res_demean_ols) ##  ## Lee-Wooldridge DiD (lwdidR) ## Design:      staggered ## Transf.:     demean ## VCE:         OLS ## -------------------------------------------------- ## Overall ATT:   0.0917 ## SE:            0.0571 ## t-stat:        1.6067 ## p-value:       0.1147 ## -------------------------------------------------- ##  ## Cohort-specific effects: ##  cohort     att      se  tstat pvalue ##    2005 0.08017 0.17305 0.4632 0.6468 ##    2006 0.06824 0.07220 0.9450 0.3503 ##    2007 0.11406 0.08998 1.2676 0.2144 ##    2008 0.14605 0.13963 1.0459 0.3042 ##    2009 0.21108 0.19105 1.1049 0.2786

title: Castle Law Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Section 7.2
code: res_demean_hc3 <- lwdid(   data          = castle,   y             = "lhomicide",   ivar          = "sid",   tvar          = "year",   gvar          = "gvar",   rolling       = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate     = "overall",   vce           = "hc3" ) print(res_demean_hc3) ##  ## Lee-Wooldridge DiD (lwdidR) ## Design:      staggered ## Transf.:     demean ## VCE:         HC3 ## -------------------------------------------------- ## Overall ATT:   0.0917 ## SE:            0.0612 ## t-stat:        1.4997 ## p-value:       0.1402 ## -------------------------------------------------- ##  ## Cohort-specific effects: ##  cohort     att      se tstat    pvalue ##    2005 0.08017 0.03215 2.493 1.884e-02 ##    2006 0.06824 0.08920 0.765 4.488e-01 ##    2007 0.11406 0.09838 1.159 2.552e-01 ##    2008 0.14605 0.08203 1.780 8.548e-02 ##    2009 0.21108 0.03550 5.946 2.115e-06

title: Castle Law Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Section 7.2
Castle Law Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Section 7.2
text: Table 7.2 Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025)
code: paper_vals <- data.frame(   Method       = c("Demeaning (OLS)", "Demeaning (HC3)", "Detrending (HC3)"),   Paper_ATT    = c(0.092, 0.092, 0.067),   Paper_SE     = c(0.057, NA, 0.055),   Paper_tstat  = c(1.61, 1.50, 1.21) )  r_vals <- data.frame(   Method     = c("Demeaning (OLS)", "Demeaning (HC3)", "Detrending (HC3)"),   R_ATT      = round(c(res_demean_ols$att_overall,                         res_demean_hc3$att_overall,                         res_detrend_hc3$att_overall), 4),   R_SE       = round(c(res_demean_ols$se_overall,                         res_demean_hc3$se_overall,                         res_detrend_hc3$se_overall), 4),   R_tstat    = round(c(res_demean_ols$tstat,                         res_demean_hc3$tstat,                         res_detrend_hc3$tstat), 3) )  results_table <- merge(paper_vals, r_vals, by = "Method") kable(results_table, caption = "Table 7.2 Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025)")

title: Castle Law Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Section 7.2
text: Cohort Effects (Equation 7.9, Lee & Wooldridge 2025)
code: res_cohort <- lwdid(   data          = castle,   y             = "lhomicide",   ivar          = "sid",   tvar          = "year",   gvar          = "gvar",   rolling       = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate     = "cohort",   vce           = "hc3" )  cat("\nCohort-specific ATT estimates:\n") ##  ## Cohort-specific ATT estimates: kable(   res_cohort$att_by_cohort[, c("cohort", "att", "se", "ci_lower", "ci_upper",                                  "tstat", "pvalue", "n_periods", "n_units")],   digits = 4,   caption = "Cohort Effects (Equation 7.9, Lee & Wooldridge 2025)" )

code: res_none <- lwdid(   data          = castle,   y             = "lhomicide",   ivar          = "sid",   tvar          = "year",   gvar          = "gvar",   rolling       = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate     = "none",   vce           = "hc3" )  cat(sprintf("\nTotal (g,r) pairs estimated: %d\n", nrow(res_none$att_by_cohort_time))) ##  ## Total (g,r) pairs estimated: 20 kable(   head(res_none$att_by_cohort_time[, c("cohort", "period", "event_time",                                          "att", "se", "pvalue")], 15),   digits = 4,   caption = "(g, r)-Specific Effects (Equation 7.8, Lee & Wooldridge 2025)" )

title: Castle Law Replication: Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Section 7.2
code:
cat("=== Numerical Verification ===

") ## === Numerical Verification === tol <- 0.001 checks <- list(   list(label = "Demeaning ATT",   got = res_demean_ols$att_overall, ref = 0.092),   list(label = "OLS SE",          got = res_demean_ols$se_overall,  ref = 0.057),   list(label = "OLS t-stat",      got = res_demean_ols$tstat,       ref = 1.607),   list(label = "HC3 t-stat",      got = res_demean_hc3$tstat,       ref = 1.50),   list(label = "Detrending ATT",  got = res_detrend_hc3$att_overall,ref = 0.067),   list(label = "Detrend HC3 t",   got = res_detrend_hc3$tstat,      ref = 1.21) )  all_pass <- TRUE for (chk in checks) {   diff  <- abs(chk$got - chk$ref)   pass  <- diff < tol   all_pass <- all_pass && pass   cat(sprintf("%-20s  got: %7.4f  ref: %5.3f  diff: %.5f  %s
",               chk$label, chk$got, chk$ref, diff,               if (pass) "PASS" else "FAIL")) } ## Demeaning ATT         got:  0.0917  ref: 0.092  diff: 0.00025  PASS ## OLS SE                got:  0.0571  ref: 0.057  diff: 0.00010  PASS ## OLS t-stat            got:  1.6067  ref: 1.607  diff: 0.00033  PASS ## HC3 t-stat            got:  1.4997  ref: 1.500  diff: 0.00026  PASS ## Detrending ATT        got:  0.0666  ref: 0.067  diff: 0.00045  PASS ## Detrend HC3 t         got:  1.2102  ref: 1.210  diff: 0.00024  PASS cat(sprintf("
All checks pass: %s
", all_pass)) ##  ## All checks pass: TRUE

title: Inference Comparison: OLS, HC3, Wild Bootstrap, Permutation
text: vignette evaluates four inference procedures available lwdidR via Monte Carlo simulation study. methods : wild bootstrap permutation tests distribution-free alternatives expected maintain correct size small N even errors non-normal, cost additional computation.

title: Inference Comparison: OLS, HC3, Wild Bootstrap, Permutation
text: consider balanced common-timing panel: NN units (half treated), Tpre=3T_{\text{pre}} = 3, Tpost=3T_{\text{post}} = 3 yit=αi+βDit+εity_{} = \alpha_i + \beta D_{} + \varepsilon_{} αi∼N(0,1)\alpha_i \sim N(0, 1) (removed demeaning) εit∼N(0,1)\varepsilon_{} \sim N(0,1) (normal) εit∼t(3)\varepsilon_{} \sim t(3) (heavy tails) β=0\beta = 0 size experiments; β=0.2\beta = 0.2 power / coverage
code: library(lwdidR) library(ggplot2)  #' Simulate one dataset and return a one-row data.frame of results #' #' @param N      Total units (N/2 treated) #' @param beta   True ATT #' @param dist   Error distribution: "normal" or "t3" #' @param method vce argument passed to lwdid() #' @param nboot  Bootstrap / permutation replications #' @param seed   RNG seed one_sim <- function(N, beta, dist, method, nboot = 199, seed = NULL) {   if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)    T_pre  <- 3L   T_post <- 3L   T_tot  <- T_pre + T_post   periods <- seq_len(T_tot)   post_periods <- seq(T_pre + 1L, T_tot)    unit_ids    <- seq_len(N)   treated_ids <- seq_len(N %/% 2L)    # Fixed effects   alpha <- stats::rnorm(N)    # Errors   eps <- if (dist == "normal") {     matrix(stats::rnorm(N * T_tot), N, T_tot)   } else {     matrix(stats::rt(N * T_tot, df = 3), N, T_tot)   }    # Outcome   D_mat <- outer(unit_ids %in% treated_ids, periods >= (T_pre + 1L), `&`) * 1L   Y_mat <- outer(alpha, rep(1, T_tot)) + beta * D_mat + eps    # Long format   # post = 1 only for treated units in post-treatment periods.   # lwdid() identifies treated units as those that ever have post == 1,   # so control units must always have post == 0.   id_vec   <- rep(unit_ids, times = T_tot)   t_vec    <- rep(periods,  each  = N)   post_vec <- as.integer(id_vec %in% treated_ids & t_vec >= (T_pre + 1L))   df <- data.frame(id = id_vec, t = t_vec, post = post_vec,                    y = as.numeric(Y_mat))    vce_arg  <- if (method == "ols") NULL else method   nboot_arg <- if (method %in% c("wildboot", "permutation")) nboot else 999L    res <- tryCatch(     lwdid(df, y = "y", ivar = "id", tvar = "t", post = "post",           rolling = "demean", vce = vce_arg,           nboot = nboot_arg, nperm = nboot_arg),     error = function(e) NULL   )    if (is.null(res)) {     return(data.frame(N = N, beta = beta, dist = dist, method = method,                       att = NA, se = NA, pvalue = NA, ci_lower = NA,                       ci_upper = NA))   }    data.frame(     N        = N,     beta     = beta,     dist     = dist,     method   = method,     att      = res$att_overall,     se       = res$se_overall,     pvalue   = res$pvalue,     ci_lower = res$ci_lower,     ci_upper = res$ci_upper   ) }

title: Inference Comparison: OLS, HC3, Wild Bootstrap, Permutation
text: run S = 500 iterations per cell. Cells defined N∈{20,50,100}N \\{20, 50, 100\}, error distribution, treatment status (β=0\beta = 0 0.20.2), four inference methods. Bootstrap permutation replications set 199 speed.
code: set.seed(42) S       <- 500L N_vals  <- c(20L, 50L, 100L) methods <- c("ols", "hc3", "wildboot", "permutation") dists   <- c("normal", "t3") betas   <- c(0, 0.2)  # Build parameter grid grid <- expand.grid(   N      = N_vals,   beta   = betas,   dist   = dists,   method = methods,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Run all simulations results_list <- vector("list", nrow(grid) * S) idx <- 0L for (row_i in seq_len(nrow(grid))) {   g <- grid[row_i, ]   for (s in seq_len(S)) {     idx <- idx + 1L     results_list[[idx]] <- one_sim(       N      = g$N,       beta   = g$beta,       dist   = g$dist,       method = g$method,       nboot  = 199L,       seed   = row_i * 10000L + s     )   } }  sim_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list) size_df  <- sim_df[sim_df$beta == 0,   ] power_df <- sim_df[sim_df$beta == 0.2, ]  size_df$reject   <- size_df$pvalue  < 0.05 power_df$reject  <- power_df$pvalue < 0.05 power_df$covered <- power_df$ci_lower <= 0.2 & 0.2 <= power_df$ci_upper  # Summary CSVs: one row per (N, dist, method) cell size_summ <- do.call(rbind, by(size_df, list(size_df$N, size_df$dist, size_df$method),   function(d) data.frame(N = d$N[1], dist = d$dist[1], method = d$method[1],     rejection_rate = mean(d$reject, na.rm = TRUE),     mean_se = mean(d$se, na.rm = TRUE), median_se = median(d$se, na.rm = TRUE),     sd_se = sd(d$se, na.rm = TRUE))))  power_summ <- do.call(rbind, by(power_df, list(power_df$N, power_df$dist, power_df$method),   function(d) data.frame(N = d$N[1], dist = d$dist[1], method = d$method[1],     rejection_rate = mean(d$reject, na.rm = TRUE),     coverage = mean(d$covered, na.rm = TRUE),     mean_se = mean(d$se, na.rm = TRUE), median_se = median(d$se, na.rm = TRUE),     sd_se = sd(d$se, na.rm = TRUE))))  dir.create("../output", showWarnings = FALSE) write.csv(size_summ,  "../output/sim_size_results.csv",  row.names = FALSE) write.csv(power_summ, "../output/sim_power_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

title: Inference Comparison: OLS, HC3, Wild Bootstrap, Permutation
text: Rejection rates H₀ (β = 0) α = 0.05. Target 0.05. Values > 0.075 indicate -rejection.
code: size_tab <- aggregate(reject ~ N + dist + method, data = size_df, FUN = mean) size_tab$reject <- round(size_tab$reject, 3) size_tab <- size_tab[order(size_tab$dist, size_tab$N, size_tab$method), ]  # Wide format size_wide <- reshape(size_tab,   timevar   = "method",   idvar     = c("N", "dist"),   direction = "wide" ) names(size_wide) <- sub("reject\\.", "", names(size_wide))  knitr::kable(size_wide,   caption = "Rejection rates under H₀ (β = 0) at α = 0.05.              Target is 0.05. Values > 0.075 indicate over-rejection.",   digits = 3, row.names = FALSE )

what: Coverage (95% CI coverage under H₁, target = 0.95)
title: Inference Comparison: OLS, HC3, Wild Bootstrap, Permutation
text: 95% CI coverage H₁ (β = 0.2). Target 0.95. Values < 0.90 indicate -coverage.
code: cov_tab <- aggregate(covered ~ N + dist + method, data = power_df, FUN = mean) cov_tab$covered <- round(cov_tab$covered, 3) cov_tab <- cov_tab[order(cov_tab$dist, cov_tab$N, cov_tab$method), ]  cov_wide <- reshape(cov_tab,   timevar   = "method",   idvar     = c("N", "dist"),   direction = "wide" ) names(cov_wide) <- sub("covered\\.", "", names(cov_wide))  knitr::kable(cov_wide,   caption = "95% CI coverage under H₁ (β = 0.2).              Target is 0.95. Values < 0.90 indicate under-coverage.",   digits = 3, row.names = FALSE )

title: Inference Comparison: OLS, HC3, Wild Bootstrap, Permutation
text: Power — rejection rate H₁ (β = 0.2) α = 0.05.
code: pow_tab <- aggregate(reject ~ N + dist + method, data = power_df, FUN = mean) pow_tab$reject <- round(pow_tab$reject, 3) pow_tab <- pow_tab[order(pow_tab$dist, pow_tab$N, pow_tab$method), ]  pow_wide <- reshape(pow_tab,   timevar   = "method",   idvar     = c("N", "dist"),   direction = "wide" ) names(pow_wide) <- sub("reject\\.", "", names(pow_wide))  knitr::kable(pow_wide,   caption = "Power — rejection rate under H₁ (β = 0.2) at α = 0.05.",   digits = 3, row.names = FALSE )

what: SE Distribution by Method and N
title: Inference Comparison: OLS, HC3, Wild Bootstrap, Permutation
text: SE distribution across simulations (β = 0.2, normal errors). Boxes summarise 500 replications. Wildboot permutation SEs reflect variability randomisation-based estimates. SE distribution heavy-tailed (t(3)) errors.
code: plot_df <- power_df[power_df$dist == "normal" & !is.na(power_df$se), ] plot_df$Method <- factor(plot_df$method,   levels = c("ols", "hc3", "wildboot", "permutation"),   labels = c("OLS", "HC3", "Wild Bootstrap", "Permutation") ) plot_df$N_label <- paste0("N = ", plot_df$N)  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Method, y = se, fill = Method)) +   geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.6, outlier.alpha = 0.4) +   facet_wrap(~ N_label, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x = NULL, y = "Standard Error",     title = "SE Distribution by Inference Method and Sample Size",     subtitle = "Normal errors, β = 0.2, 500 replications per cell"   ) +   theme_bw(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "none",         axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) plot_df2 <- power_df[power_df$dist == "t3" & !is.na(power_df$se), ] plot_df2$Method <- factor(plot_df2$method,   levels = c("ols", "hc3", "wildboot", "permutation"),   labels = c("OLS", "HC3", "Wild Bootstrap", "Permutation") ) plot_df2$N_label <- paste0("N = ", plot_df2$N)  ggplot(plot_df2, aes(x = Method, y = se, fill = Method)) +   geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.6, outlier.alpha = 0.4) +   facet_wrap(~ N_label, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     x = NULL, y = "Standard Error",     title = "SE Distribution by Inference Method and Sample Size",     subtitle = "t(3) errors, β = 0.2, 500 replications per cell"   ) +   theme_bw(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "none",         axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

title: Inference Comparison: OLS, HC3, Wild Bootstrap, Permutation
text: simulation illustrates three key points: Size control small N. N=20N = 20, homoskedastic OLS t-test HC3 can -reject errors heavy-tailed. Wild bootstrap permutation inference maintain empirical size closer nominal 5% level using finite-sample randomisation distribution rather asymptotic critical values. Power. methods achieve similar power N=100N = 100. small N, distribution-free methods may sacrifice modest power exchange correct size. Coverage. Coverage tracks size results: methods -reject H0H_0 exhibit -coverage H1H_1. Recommendation. Use vce = "hc3" default. N≲30N \lesssim 30 errors suspected non-normal, prefer vce = "wildboot" vce = "permutation" least nboot = 499 replications.

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: R implementation Lee & Wooldridge (2025) panel difference--differences estimator via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations.

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("xiangao/lwdidR")

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: lwdidR implements estimator : Lee, Y., & Wooldridge, J. M. (2025). simple panel data approach difference--differences general treatment patterns. key idea: residualise unit’s outcome using pre-treatment observations (demean detrend), run pooled cross-sectional OLS. sidesteps Callaway-Sant’Anna / Sun-Abraham aggregation issues allowing flexible control unit-specific trends. Supports: - Common-timing staggered adoption designs - Transformation methods: demean, detrend, demeanq, detrendq - Standard errors: homoskedastic OLS, HC1, HC3, cluster-robust - Cohort-specific ATTs (g,r)-level event-study estimates

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
code: library(lwdidR)  # Load bundled Castle Doctrine dataset (50 US states, 2000-2010) castle <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "castle.csv", package = "lwdidR")) castle$gvar <- castle$effyear castle$gvar[is.na(castle$gvar) | castle$gvar == 0] <- NA  # Staggered design: demeaning with HC3 SEs res <- lwdid(castle, "lhomicide", "sid", "year",              gvar = "gvar", rolling = "demean", vce = "hc3") print(res) Lee-Wooldridge DiD (lwdidR) Design:      staggered Transf.:     demean VCE:         HC3 -------------------------------------------------- Overall ATT:   0.0917 SE:            0.0612 t-stat:        1.4995 p-value:       0.1376

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: Castle Doctrine laws log homicide rates: results match paper Table 7.2 within tolerance 0.001. See vignette("castle_law") full replication.

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: Full documentation: https://xiangao.github.io/lwdidR/

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: Overall ATT (staggered): delta-method average cohort ATTs. Instead, pooled cross-sectional regression: - Treated unit : outcome = ydot_postavg cohort g - Never-treated unit : outcome = weighted average ydot_postavg across cohorts - Single OLS N_treated + N_never_treated units matches Python reference implementation yields SE ≈ 0.061 (0.051 naive averaging).

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: Lee, Y., & Wooldridge, J. M. (2025). simple panel data approach difference--differences general treatment patterns. Working paper.

what: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
title: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
text: Computes ydot (residualised outcome), ydot_postavg (post-treatment average ydot per unit), marks firstpost cross-section.

title: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
code: apply_transform(df, y, ivar, tindex, post, rolling, tpost1, season_var = NULL)

title: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
text: df Data frame (long format, one row per unit-period) y Outcome column name ivar Unit identifier column name tindex Integer time index column name post Binary post-treatment indicator column name (0=pre, 1=post) rolling Transformation method: "demean", "detrend", "demeanq", "detrendq" tpost1 First post-treatment period index season_var Column name seasonal indicator (required demeanq/detrendq)

title: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
text: Data frame added columns: ydot, ydot_postavg, firstpost

what: Estimate ATT via OLS on firstpost cross-section (uses ydot_postavg) — estimate_att

title: Estimate ATT via OLS on firstpost cross-section (uses ydot_postavg) — estimate_att

what: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
text: Main entry point implementing Lee & Wooldridge (2025) panel difference--differences estimator. Supports common-timing staggered adoption designs. unit's outcome residualised using pre-treatment observations running pooled cross-sectional OLS.

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
code: lwdid(   data,   y,   ivar,   tvar,   gvar = NULL,   post = NULL,   rolling = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate = "overall",   vce = NULL,   cluster_var = NULL,   controls = NULL,   season_var = NULL,   nboot = 999,   nperm = 999,   vce_inner = "hc3" )

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
text: data long-format panel data frame (one row per unit-period). y Character. Name outcome column. ivar Character. Name unit identifier column. tvar Character. Name calendar time column (numeric integer). gvar Character NULL. Name first-treatment-year column staggered designs. Units value 0 NA treated never-treated. Set NULL (default) common-timing designs (supply post instead). post Character NULL. Name binary post-treatment indicator column (0 = pre, 1 = post). Required gvar = NULL; ignored otherwise. rolling Character. Transformation method applied unit's pre-treatment observations: "demean" Subtract unit's pre-period mean (default). "detrend" Remove linear trend fitted pre-periods. "demeanq" Seasonal demeaning; requires season_var. "detrendq" Seasonal detrending; requires season_var. control_group Character. Control group staggered designs: "never_treated" (default) "not_yet_treated". aggregate Character. Aggregation level staggered designs: "overall" (default), "cohort", "none" (returns (g,r) pairs). vce Character NULL. Variance-covariance estimator: NULL (homoskedastic OLS), "hc1", "hc3", "cluster", "wildboot" (wild cluster bootstrap), "permutation" (randomisation inference). last two distribution-free recommended small N. cluster_var Character NULL. Column name clustering; required vce = "cluster". controls Character vector NULL. Names time-invariant control variables include cross-sectional regression. season_var Character NULL. Column name seasonal indicator (required rolling = "demeanq" "detrendq"). nboot Integer. Number bootstrap replications vce = "wildboot" (default 999). nperm Integer. Number permutations vce = "permutation" (default 999). vce_inner Character. Inner variance estimator used computing observed t-statistic inside wild bootstrap (default "hc3").

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
text: object class "lwdid", list containing: design "staggered" "common_timing". att_overall Estimated overall ATT. se_overall Standard error overall ATT. tstat t-statistic. pvalue Two-sided p-value. att_by_cohort Data frame cohort-specific ATTs (staggered ). att_by_cohort_time Data frame (g,r)-specific ATTs (staggered ). att_by_period Data frame period-specific ATTs (common timing ). ci_lower, ci_upper 95% confidence interval bounds (common timing ). N Sample size first post-treatment period (common timing ).

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
text: Lee, Y., & Wooldridge, J. M. (2025). simple panel data approach difference--differences general treatment patterns.

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
code: # Load bundled Castle Doctrine dataset castle <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "castle.csv", package = "lwdidR")) castle$gvar <- castle$effyear castle$gvar[is.na(castle$gvar) | castle$gvar == 0] <- NA  # Staggered design with demeaning and HC3 standard errors res <- lwdid(castle, "lhomicide", "sid", "year",              gvar = "gvar", rolling = "demean", vce = "hc3") print(res) #>  #> Lee-Wooldridge DiD (lwdidR) #> Design:      staggered #> Transf.:     demean #> VCE:         HC3 #> -------------------------------------------------- #> Overall ATT:   0.0917 #> SE:            0.0612 #> t-stat:        1.4997 #> p-value:       0.1402 #> -------------------------------------------------- #>  #> Cohort-specific effects: #>  cohort     att      se tstat    pvalue #>    2005 0.08017 0.03215 2.493 1.884e-02 #>    2006 0.06824 0.08920 0.765 4.488e-01 #>    2007 0.11406 0.09838 1.159 2.552e-01 #>    2008 0.14605 0.08203 1.780 8.548e-02 #>    2009 0.21108 0.03550 5.946 2.115e-06 #>

what: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered
title: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered
text: Python-equivalent algorithm: cohort g: Apply transformation using pre-periods (t < g) units Compute ydot_postavg(g,) per unit Overall ATT: pooled regression Treated unit : Y_bar_i = ydot_postavg(g_i, ) Never-treated unit : Y_bar_i = sum_g w_g * ydot_postavg(g,) OLS: Y_bar ~ D (D=1 ever treated) N_treat + N_NT units Cohort ATT_g: per-cohort regression Y_bar(g,) = ydot_postavg(g,) cohort g units + NT controls OLS: Y_bar_g ~ D_g

title: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered
code: lwdid_staggered(   data,   y,   ivar,   tvar,   gvar,   rolling = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate = "overall",   vce = NULL,   cluster_var = NULL,   controls = NULL,   season_var = NULL,   nboot = 999,   nperm = 999,   vce_inner = "hc3" )

title: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered

title: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered
text: Named list att_overall, att_by_cohort, att_by_cohort_time

title: Print an lwdid object — print.lwdid
text: Displays compact summary estimation results including design type, transformation method, VCE, overall ATT, SE, t-statistic, p-value. staggered designs also prints cohort-specific effects.

title: Summarise an lwdid object — summary.lwdid
text: Calls print.lwdid() additionally prints period-specific ATTs (common-timing design) first 10 (g, r)-specific ATTs (staggered design).

title: lwdidR 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Implements Lee & Wooldridge (2025) estimator via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations Supports common-timing staggered adoption designs Transformation methods: demean, detrend, demeanq, detrendq Standard errors: homoskedastic OLS, HC1, HC3, cluster-robust (manual sandwich) Staggered design: overall pooled ATT, cohort-specific ATT, (g,r)-level effects Bundles Castle Doctrine dataset replication paper Section 7.2 Vignette replicating numerical results Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Table 7.2
